brief_justification: This is a qualitative single-country case study of domestic coalitions in Brazil during FTAA negotiations. It uses historical and process-tracing analysis drawing on political economy theories (Rogowski, Katzenstein, Milner) to explain why industrialists and trade unions failed to align despite convergent trade policy preferences. The paper contains no quantitative data analysis, no tables, no figures, and no equations. It makes an implicit causal argument that trade liberalization altered the priorities of labor and capital, preventing coalition formation, but does so through qualitative narrative rather than statistical methods.
